articles.title: Riskified director Shachar Erez sells $870,811 in shares By Investing.com
articles.summary: Shachar Erez, a director at Riskified (NASDAQ:RSKD), recently sold Class A Ordinary Shares worth $870,811 through Qumra Capital entities. These sales, conducted on June 18 and June 22, 2026, were part of a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan and involved 176,804 shares at prices between $4.85 and $5.00. The transactions occurred while the stock was near its 52-week low, and follow other company news including a new share repurchase authorization and mixed Q1 2026 earnings.

articles.title: QURE Stock Soars As FDA Backs Accelerated Huntington’s Therapy Path
articles.summary: uniQure N.V. (QURE) stock has seen a significant surge after the FDA indicated that three-year Phase I/II data for its AMT-130 gene therapy could support an accelerated approval pathway for Huntington’s disease. This regulatory clarity has led to a re-rating of the stock, with major banks raising price targets and upgrading their recommendations. Despite high financial risk metrics typical of biotech, QURE's balance sheet provides runway, making it a high-momentum regulatory play for traders.

articles.title: June 29, 2026 Deadline: Join Class Action Lawsuit Against POET Technologies Inc. (POET) - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
articles.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP has filed a securities class action lawsuit against POET Technologies Inc. (NASDAQ:POET) on behalf of investors who suffered losses between April 1, 2026, and April 27, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that POET Technologies made false statements concerning its tax status as a potential passive foreign investment company and a violation of a non-disclosure agreement by a defendant. Investors are encouraged to contact Levi & Korsinsky by the June 29, 2026 deadline to explore potential recovery.

articles.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent signaled confidence in newly installed Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh during remarks Tuesday, and predicted that inflation will retreat as the Iran conflict subsides.

articles.title: Japan Post Holdings sells $3.25m Aflac (NYSE:AFL) stock By Investing.com
articles.summary: Japan Post Holdings, a 10% owner of Aflac Inc., sold approximately $3.25 million worth of Aflac common stock on June 22, 2026. The sales involved two separate transactions at weighted average prices of $116.57 and $117.46 per share, respectively. Following these sales, Japan Post Holdings indirectly retains beneficial ownership of over 50 million Aflac shares.

articles.title: uniQure prices $225M public offering at $45.50 per share By Investing.com
articles.summary: uniQure N.V. (NASDAQ:QURE) announced it priced an underwritten public offering of 4,945,055 ordinary shares at $45.50 per share, aiming to generate approximately $225 million in gross proceeds. The gene therapy company also granted underwriters a 30-day option to purchase additional shares and expects the offering to close around June 25, 2026. Despite a 245% stock surge and a market cap of $3.09 billion, InvestingPro analysis suggests the company is overvalued and remains unprofitable with an EPS of -$3.49.

articles.title: The Summerwood at Harmony from PulteGroup Inc. - flexible floorplan with home office and covered lan
articles.summary: PulteGroup Inc. is offering "The Summerwood at Harmony," a single-story home model in St. Cloud, Florida, targeting growing families, downsizers, and buyers seeking flexible living spaces. This model, part of PulteGroup's Sunbelt master-planned communities strategy, features an open-concept layout, a flex room that can serve as a home office or third bedroom, and a covered lanai. Priced in the low-to-mid $400,000s, it aims to balance compact living with modern amenities within the Harmony community.

articles.title: Sanrio Shares Seesaw After Full-Year Forecast Beats Estimate
articles.summary: Sanrio Co. shares briefly jumped the most since February after the Japanese Hello Kitty owner forecast full-year operating profit above analyst estimates.

articles.title: uniQure locks in $225M from a new share sale
articles.summary: uniQure (Nasdaq: QURE) has announced the pricing of an upsized underwritten public offering of 4,945,055 ordinary shares at $45.50 per share, aiming to raise approximately $225 million in gross proceeds. The offering grants underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 741,758 shares, with closing expected around June 25, 2026. This move, while providing capital, may lead to dilution for existing shareholders.

articles.title: Shareholders support PRA Group (NASDAQ: PRAA) equity plan, pay and auditor
articles.summary: PRA Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: PRAA) shareholders approved several key proposals at their 2026 Annual Meeting. They voted to increase the share limit of the 2022 Omnibus Incentive Plan by 3,500,000 shares, elected all nominated directors, ratified Ernst & Young LLP as the independent auditor for 2026, and approved executive compensation in an advisory vote. These results indicate strong stockholder confidence in the company's governance and compensation strategies.

FedEx Corporation (FDX) Q4 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
Indonesia Gets Some Breathing Room as MSCI Delays Downgrade Decision
articles.summary: MSCI has deferred its decision on downgrading Indonesia's market status, allowing the country to retain its emerging-market classification for now. This delay provides a significant reprieve for Indonesia, whose market has been struggling with declining investor confidence and whose currency and stock market are among Asia's weakest performers this year. Analysts had previously warned that a downgrade to frontier-market status could have triggered substantial capital outflows.

articles.summary: Market expert Raja Venkatraman shares his top stock picks for 24 June. Here’s his technical outlook and trade strategy.

articles.summary: Siya and her lover Chetan were arrested for the murder of Ketan Agrawal, stemming from a complicated relationship and previous attempts on Ketan's life. Police investigations uncovered inconsistencies, indicating a potential conspiracy involving Siya's family and premeditated actions against Ketan.

articles.title: Alphabet joins Dow as S&P swaps out Verizon over weak stock impact - CHOSUNBIZ
articles.summary: Alphabet Class C (GOOG) is set to join the Dow Jones Industrial Average, replacing Verizon (VZ), effective June 18. This change is due to Verizon's low stock price, which limited its impact on the price-weighted index. Alphabet's inclusion is expected to increase the Dow's technology sector exposure and provide a more significant influence on the index's movements.

articles.title: J. M. Smucker (SJM) Stock Could Be 8.7% Undervalued After Earnings Beat And Stronger Outlook
articles.summary: J. M. Smucker (SJM) is gaining investor attention after an earnings beat and an improved fiscal 2027 EPS forecast, driven by strong demand for its products. While a discounted cash flow model suggests the stock is 8.7% undervalued at $121.06, a sales-based valuation indicates it might be expensive. Investors are advised to examine key rewards and warning signs, and consider the underlying narratives and risks, such as volatile coffee costs or weaker demand, before making investment decisions.

articles.title: Is Kraft Heinz (KHC) Turning Regional Reshuffle Into a Coherent Brand and Execution Strategy?
articles.summary: Kraft Heinz recently reorganized its global operations into three regions and launched new products, aiming to refresh brands and sharpen execution after mixed performance. This article explores how the new structure, especially the consolidation of Emerging Markets, might affect its investment narrative, which hinges on brand relevance, margin protection, and stable earnings. While some see potential for growth in emerging markets, others remain cautious about innovation and consumer preference shifts, especially in the North American market.

articles.title: Korean Stocks Rebound After 10% Rout With Focus Back on Earnings
articles.summary: South Korean stocks rebounded from a 10% rout, with investors refocusing on chipmakers’ earnings after what many saw as a selloff driven by technical factors.

articles.title: Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL.US) to Replace Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ.US) as DJIA Constituent Effective Next Mon
articles.summary: S&P Global announced that Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL.US) will replace Verizon Communications Inc. (VZ.US) as a constituent of the DJIA, effective next Monday (29th). This change aims to increase the weighting of large-cap technology stocks and enhance the DJIA’s exposure to AI, cloud computing infrastructure, and advertising. Following the announcement, Alphabet Inc. saw a slight increase in after-hours trading, while Verizon Communications Inc. experienced a minor dip.

articles.title: CBRE Group enters new $1 billion credit agreement with Wells Fargo
articles.summary: CBRE Group (NYSE:CBRE) has secured a new 364-day, $1 billion senior unsecured revolving credit agreement with Wells Fargo Bank. This facility replaces a maturing agreement and features interest rates tied to Term SOFR or a base rate plus a variable margin based on CBRE's credit rating. The company's financial health is robust, with a strong Altman Z-Score and a moderate debt level, according to InvestingPro data, and recent financial results show an earnings beat and successful capital raises.

articles.title: Is IDBI Bank stock undervalued? It all hinges on the government’s potential stake sale.
articles.summary: While the bank is classified as private on paper, it still trades at heavily discounted public-sector multiples. But if the government pulls off its long-awaited divestment, a sharp re-rating could finally be unlocked.

articles.title: PE-backed firms ride NSE, Jio tailwind to launch IPO roadshows
articles.summary: Investment bankers are preparing IPO roadshows for at least five to six PE-backed firms, including InCred, Dhoot Transmission and Integris Medtech, riding on improved liquidity sentiment after National Stock Exchange and Jio's DRHP filings.&nbsp;

articles.title: Philippine telecoms giant PLDT files for first data centre REIT IPO
articles.summary: Philippine telecom giant PLDT Inc. has filed a registration statement for the initial public offering (IPO) of VITRO REIT, which could raise up to P24.2 billion ($397 million). This proposed listing would be the Philippines' first digital infrastructure REIT, coming amidst increasing demand for data center development in Southeast Asia.

articles.title: House clears affordability bill to lower housing costs, sending it to Trump's desk for approval: Here's what we know
articles.summary: The US House cleared a massive housing affordability bill on Tuesday (local time) aimed at boosting the supply of homes and cracking down on large investors buying up houses. The bill received 358-32 votes in favour and has now gone to Trump for approval

articles.title: Novavax (NVAX) director adds RSUs, options and exercises prior RSU grant
articles.summary: Novavax director Richard Douglas reported equity compensation activity, exercising 18,880 restricted stock units (RSUs) and receiving new grants of 14,180 RSUs and a stock option for 21,270 shares. These transactions were compensation-related and not open-market trades. Following these actions, his direct holdings of Novavax common stock increased to 73,220 shares.

articles.title: Can Plug Power (NASDAQ:PLUG) Lead the Next Penny Stock Rally?
articles.summary: Plug Power (NASDAQ:PLUG) has garnered significant market attention due to active trading in hydrogen-related stocks, benefiting from small-cap strength, shifts in energy markets, and the growing interest in clean power. The company specializes in developing hydrogen fuel cell systems and associated clean-power infrastructure for commercial use. It operates within the clean-energy sector, with a specific focus on hydrogen fuel cell technology.

articles.title: The XR12 articulating boom lift from United Rentals Inc. - 40-foot reach for tight urban jobs
articles.summary: The article introduces the XR12 articulating boom lift from United Rentals, highlighting its 40-foot reach and maneuverability for tight urban job sites. It emphasizes the integrated telematics for fleet management and its contribution to United Rentals' stock performance. The XR12 is presented as a compact solution for contractors, facility managers, and event crews in constrained urban environments.

articles.title: PEDEVCO (PED) grants time- and performance-based RSUs to its Chief Commercial Officer
articles.summary: PEDEVCO Corp. (PED) has granted Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) to its Chief Commercial Officer, Jody D. Crook. The awards include 16,050 time-based RSUs vesting over three years and 7,020 performance-based RSUs tied to the company's total shareholder return against a peer group for fiscal years 2026-2028, with cliff-vesting on December 31, 2028. Following these grants, Crook directly holds 33,417 shares of common stock in addition to the new RSU awards.

articles.title: Amgen vs. Iovance Biotherapeutics: Which Healthcare Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
articles.summary: This article compares Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) and Iovance Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:IOVA) as investment opportunities for 2026, weighing the stability of a large pharmaceutical company against the high-growth potential of an innovative biotech firm. While Iovance offers cutting-edge cancer therapies and rapid revenue growth, it remains unprofitable, whereas Amgen provides a diversified revenue base, consistent profitability, and a dividend. The author ultimately recommends Amgen due to its dependability and established financial health despite biosimilar competition, reserving Iovance for investors with higher risk tolerance.

articles.title: Equity awards to Novavax (NVAX) director: 14,180 RSUs and 21,270 options
articles.summary: Novavax director John W. Shiver was granted 14,180 restricted stock units (RSUs) and stock options on 21,270 shares with an exercise price of $9.13, as part of his board compensation. These equity awards, made under the company's Amended and Restated 2015 Stock Incentive Plan, are compensation-related grants rather than open-market transactions. Both the RSUs and options are set to vest on the first anniversary of the June 18, 2026 grant date, contingent upon his continued service on the board.

articles.title: 3 US Regional Bank Stocks With Dividends And Balance Sheet Support
articles.summary: As technology stocks face pressure, investors are shifting focus to value stocks with lower P/E and P/B ratios, steady dividends, and strong balance sheets. This article highlights three regional bank stocks—Fulton Financial (FULT), WesBanco (WSBC), and Bank OZK (OZK)—that offer dividends and balance sheet support, making them attractive amidst the current market sentiment. Each bank combines traditional financial services with specific strengths like profitability, dividend yields, and potential undervaluation, though they also carry their unique risks such as credit quality concerns or commercial real estate exposure.

articles.title: Amgen vs. Iovance Biotherapeutics: Which Healthcare Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
articles.summary: This article compares Amgen (NASDAQ:AMGN) and Iovance Biotherapeutics (NASDAQ:IOVA) as investment options for 2026, highlighting Amgen's stability and diversified revenue against Iovance's high-growth potential in innovative cancer therapies. While Iovance shows rapid revenue growth, it remains unprofitable, making Amgen the preferred choice for its steadiness, consistent financial performance, and dividend payments despite biosimilar competition. The author concludes that Amgen offers a more dependable, long-term investment.

articles.title: What NiSource (NI)'s Amazon-Backed Data Center Power Deal Means For Shareholders
articles.summary: NiSource recently secured approval from the Indiana Utility Regulatory Commission for agreements with Amazon to power new data centers, a deal that shifts infrastructure costs to data center customers, potentially saving existing customers US$1.4 billion and streamlining future large-load projects. This strategy, also seen with an Alphabet subsidiary, aims to convert rising large-load demand into stable earnings while emphasizing the importance of favorable regulatory outcomes for NiSource's substantial capital plan and future returns. While these developments support NiSource's investment narrative, investors should be mindful of potential risks from regulatory delays or disapprovals.

articles.title: Insider sale: NEXGEL (NXGL) CEO sells 11,000 common shares
articles.summary: NEXGEL (NXGL) CEO Adam R. Levy reported two open-market sales, disposing of a total of 11,000 common shares on June 18, 2026, and June 23, 2026, at weighted average prices of approximately $0.5921 and $0.5933 per share, respectively. Following these transactions, Levy directly holds 322,692 shares of NEXGEL common stock. The filing, categorized as neutral in impact and sentiment, provides details of the sales and remaining holdings for transparency.

articles.title: Gold Drops Below $4,100 as Tech-Led Selloff Spurs Liquidation
articles.summary: Gold extended a decline, as a tech-led selloff on Wall Street prompted investors to cut bullion holdings to cover losses elsewhere in their portfolios.

articles.title: Novavax (NVAX) director granted RSUs and stock options in new equity awards
articles.summary: Novavax director Charles W. Newton was granted 14,180 restricted stock units (RSUs) and stock options for 21,270 shares with an exercise price of $9.13 per share on June 18, 2026. These equity awards, part of his compensation, will vest 100% on the first anniversary of the grant date, contingent on his continued service on the Board of Directors. The grants were made under the company’s Amended and Restated 2015 Stock Incentive Plan and were reported in a Form 4 filing.

articles.title: The Ruger AR-556 MPR from Sturm, Ruger & Co. - longer barrel, smoother shooting and surprising versa
articles.summary: This article details the Ruger AR-556 MPR, a 5.56 rifle designed for smoother shooting and versatility, featuring an 18-inch barrel, free-floating handguard, and adjustable stock. It positions the MPR as a mid-priced, feature-rich option for shooters and discusses its impact on Sturm, Ruger & Co. (RGR) shares. The rifle aims to bridge the gap between basic carbines and more expensive custom-shop models, offering enhanced accuracy and recoil management for various shooting activities.

articles.title: AdaptHealth shareholders elect directors and approve proposals at annual meeting
articles.summary: AdaptHealth Corp. shareholders approved all proposals at their annual meeting, including the election of nine directors for one-year terms. They also ratified KPMG LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, and approved the compensation of named executive officers. These decisions follow AdaptHealth's recent first-quarter 2026 earnings report, which indicated a revenue beat but an EPS miss.

articles.summary: China is choking shipments of some critical minerals to Japan, a slowdown that’s hurting companies and prompting calls for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to find a diplomatic off-ramp with Beijing.

articles.title: Huntington Bancshares Inc. stock outperforms competitors on strong trading day
articles.summary: Huntington Bancshares Inc. (HBAN) shares rose 2.05% to $17.40 on a tough trading day where the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced declines. This marks the third consecutive day of gains for Huntington Bancshares, showcasing its outperformance compared to the broader market. The stock’s positive movement occurred despite a general downturn in the stock market.
